summary: Security Hardening Testing Complete - All 14 backend tests passed (100%). Frontend login flow verified working. All security features implemented correctly: Security Headers, CORS, NoSQL Injection Protection, Account Lockout, Refresh Token Rotation, Input Sanitization, and Logout with Token Revocation.

issues: Minor: Console warning about chart dimensions (-1x-1) on initial load - cosmetic only

backend_api_tests: Security Headers: X-Frame-Options DENY present on /api/health
Security Headers: X-Content-Type-Options nosniff present
Security Headers: X-XSS-Protection 1; mode=block present
Security Headers: Referrer-Policy present
Security Headers: Content-Security-Policy with frame-ancestors present
Security Headers: Strict-Transport-Security (HSTS) present
Security Headers: Permissions-Policy present
Security Headers: Cache-Control no-store on auth endpoints
CORS: Access-Control-Allow-Origin header present
Login returns both token AND refresh_token
POST /api/auth/refresh returns new token + new refresh_token (rotation)
POST /api/auth/refresh rejects revoked refresh tokens with 401
POST /api/auth/refresh rejects invalid refresh tokens with 401
POST /api/auth/logout revokes refresh tokens and returns ok
Account lockout: HTTP 423 after 5 failed login attempts
NoSQL injection: {email: {$gt: ''}} rejected with 400/422
NoSQL injection: {password: {$ne: ''}} rejected with 400/422
Input sanitization: $ operator keys in device-report rejected with 400
Input sanitization: Nested $ operators in device-report rejected with 400
WebSocket authentication: ?token=JWT query parameter supported
frontend_ui_tests: Login page loads correctly with email/password inputs
Dashboard loads after login with 'Login effettuato' toast
WebSocket connects with token query parameter

seed_data_creation: No new seed data created. Used existing admin account and API key.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Security hardening features fully verified. All security headers present on API responses. Refresh token rotation working (old token revoked, new one issued). Account lockout triggers after 5 failed attempts (HTTP 423). NoSQL injection patterns blocked. Input sanitization blocks $ operator keys. WebSocket accepts ?token=JWT for authentication. IMPORTANT: After testing account lockout, always clear lockout state using the clear_lockout() function in test_security_hardening.py or run: python3 -c "import pymongo;c=pymongo.MongoClient('mongodb://localhost:27017');d=c['test_database'];d.login_attempts.delete_many({'success':False});d.users.update_many({},{'$set':{'locked':False},'$unset':{'locked_at':'','unlock_at':''}})"

verification_results.account_lockout: Account locked (HTTP 423) after 5 failed attempts - VERIFIED
